title: Llama 3
description: Meta's open-weight Llama-family decoder-only transformer release, spanning dense and instruction-tuned checkpoints with long-context support.
openingSummary: Llama 3 is a Meta Llama-family, text-focused, decoder-only transformer model family. It reads text tokens and generates text tokens through autoregressive next-token prediction, and the release made open-weight checkpoints at large scale and long context a mainstream reference point.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Llama 3 is Meta's open-weight language model family built around a decoder-only transformer stack. The release spans multiple dense sizes and instruction-tuned variants, with the largest public checkpoint reaching 405 billion parameters and supporting context windows up to 128K tokens.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: Llama 3 reads text tokens and produces text tokens. A prompt can be plain text, a chat transcript, or a long document, and the model continues from that context one token at a time through autoregressive generation. Instruction-tuned variants use the same token interface for assistant-style replies, while long-context checkpoints keep earlier tokens available across much longer prompts.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: At a high level, Llama 3 follows the familiar decoder-only transformer pattern: token embeddings enter a repeated stack of causal attention and feed-forward blocks, then a linear head produces next-token probabilities. Meta's public description emphasizes grouped-query attention, rotary position embeddings, RMSNorm, and a SwiGLU feed-forward block inside that stack.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: These registry-linked modules are the main moving parts to understand before reading later Llama-family releases. Grouped-query attention and causal attention carry context efficiently, RoPE marks token positions, RMSNorm stabilizes the repeated stack, and SwiGLU handles the per-token feed-forward transformation.

training.title: Training
training.body: Public Meta sources describe broad text pretraining followed by post-training work that turns base checkpoints into instruction-following assistants. The pretraining stage learns next-token prediction over large text mixtures, and later alignment and instruction tuning shape how the same stack responds in chat and tool-use settings.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: This page is an explainer and reference sheet for how Llama 3 works, not a benchmark leaderboard or model-ranking page. Use it to understand the family architecture, training story, and long-context significance; use dedicated benchmark sites when you need score comparisons across models.
